title: Protected variables, branches, and exposure rules
description: Protected CI/CD variables are exposed only to pipelines on protected branches/tags; unprotected ones reach every branch and fork MR.
body: Use when deciding how a secret is stored or why a job cannot see it. A variable marked Protected is injected only into pipelines running on a protected branch or protected tag; an unprotected variable is injected into pipelines on any branch, including feature branches and (depending on settings) fork merge-request pipelines. Masking only scrubs log output; it does not limit which pipelines receive the value. Do not assume masking implies access control or that a feature branch should see production secrets. Actions: protect secrets, protect the branches that deploy, and scope variables to environments. Validation signals include a non-protected branch pipeline failing to read a protected variable. Risk boundary: a production secret left unprotected is readable by any branch and is a blocking exposure.
